name: Red Stakes Golf
about: Red Stakes Golf is a technology-focused provider of golf simulators and launch monitors, catering to both home users and serious golfers. The company offers a range of hardware products, including the premium RSG Pro and the more accessible RSG One launch monitors, along with lifetime software memberships. Their core value proposition is delivering Tour-level accuracy and features at a significantly lower cost than competitors like Trackman and Foresight, with a focus on measured spin rates and professional-grade data. Their RSG Club ecosystem provides a connected online experience for competitive play, leaderboards, and tournaments. The business is built around a direct-to-consumer sales model, emphasizing a 365-day guarantee and American manufacturing for its high-end Pro series hardware.
why_golfers_like_it: Delivers Tour-level accuracy and measured spin rates at a fraction of the cost of premium competitors
Offers a lifetime software deal for the RSG Club, eliminating annual subscription fees for committed users
Provides a robust online community with leaderboards and competitive events for engaging gameplay
Features hardware designed for small spaces, making it accessible for home setups
Boasts a 365-day guarantee, reflecting strong confidence in product quality and customer satisfaction

food_and_drink.notes: No on-site food service is mentioned.
No on-site alcohol service is mentioned.

simulator_hardware.notes: Overhead launch monitor with dual camera technology, professional-grade accuracy, engineered and assembled in America.
Down-the-line launch monitor with single camera technology, professional-grade accuracy, center-strike safety, priced at $3,000.

faq.question: Do I need my own clubs to play?
faq.answer: The content does not specify. For the best experience with launch monitors, using your own clubs is recommended to ensure accurate data and familiar feel.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The provided text does not mention a policy on outside food or drinks. As this appears to be a retail and fitting center, it is advisable to contact the venue directly to confirm their policy.

faq.question: How long does it typically take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The content does not specify an average time for 18 holes. Golf simulator times can vary widely, typically ranging from 1.5 to 3 hours depending on simulator efficiency and player pace.

faq.question: Is this venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes. The website offers features like family-friendly pricing bundles (RSG CLUB + RSG ONE), and the platform itself is described as accessible for 'everyday golfers' and those looking to have fun, suggesting an environment suitable for juniors and families.
